title: Professional Photographer Capturing Rioplatense Waters in the Coastal Capital of Buenos Aires Province Argentina
description: Buenos Aires, Buenos Aires Province: Samuel Jeffery skillfully frames a shot of the sprawling Rio de la Plata from the vantage point of a sailing vessel. The natural light highlights the focused concentration of the photographer as he navigates the technical complexities of capturing the vast, shimmering expanse of the estuary. Against the backdrop of a cloud-dappled blue sky, the rhythmic motion of the water creates a dynamic frame for this coastal excursion. The structural geometry of the boat’s rigging contrasts with the fluid, shifting patterns of the wind-swept waves below, reflecting the interplay between maritime technology and the raw beauty of the Argentine coastline. His posture conveys a deep immersion into the creative process, isolating the immediate environment to emphasize the interplay of light and horizon. This quiet moment of observation underscores the dedication required to document the diverse landscapes that define the identity of the region. Through the lens, the photographer bridges the gap between the urban proximity of the capital and the expansive nature of the surrounding waterway. Such imagery is vital for understanding how the coastal geography of the Rio de la Plata shapes the daily life, culture, and recreational spirit of those inhabiting this massive, bustling port city. This visual documentation serves as an essential record of the intersection between human curiosity and the vast, untamed waters that have historically anchored the growth of the Argentine capital. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
